ksa_text: Knowledge of AI explainability and interpretability methods, including SHAP values, LIME, partial dependence plots, counterfactual explanations, attention visualization, and the appropriate application of each method type by model class and explanation requirement.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of test procedures, principles, and methodologies (e.g., Capabilities and Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)).

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of language processing tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of language processing tools and techniques

Knowledge of test procedures, principles, and methodologies (e.g., Capabilities and Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)).

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI Governance KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.397) to justify inclusion for the Director of Data Quality. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Director of Data Quality roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for Director of Data Quality roles increasingly include AI Governance competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for Director of Data Quality now include AI Governance competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. AI Governance knowledge KSA is relevant to the Director of Data Quality based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
